key_insight: GrapNet treats the neural network itself as an editable graph program, so structural changes like adding, deleting, freezing, or rerouting relations become first-class operations.
engineering_relevance: This makes neural models easier to inspect, modify, and deploy across different execution backends, which is valuable for engineering workflows that need controlled structure and reproducible behavior.
method: Child-owned graph nodes store trainable allocations aligned with outgoing relations.
Structural rules and execution policies are separated from the node core, enabling growth, freezing, grouping, routing, and dense lowering.
Conventional modules can feed into GrapNet through a vector-valued parent interface.
evidence_signal: In matched ten-seed studies, GrapNet improved seen-class accuracy on Split Fashion-MNIST by 12.08 points over a larger MLP+ER and improved a frozen-encoder Split CIFAR-10 head by 3.81 points, with reported statistical significance.
watch_item: The approach depends on careful bookkeeping when topology changes, since deleting a relation must also update the corresponding allocation coordinate and execution view.
paper_title: GrapNet: A Programmable Dynamic-Architecture Neural Graph Substrate
